message:
fix(func): correct total_changes() accumulation, weekday modifier semantics, and timediff carry logic

Three conformance fixes in the built-in function library:

builtins.rs:
- Add cumulative TOTAL_CHANGES thread-local counter that accumulates
  across set_last_changes() calls. TotalChangesFunc now returns this
  cumulative value instead of the per-statement LAST_CHANGES value.
- Add get_total_changes(), reset_total_changes() (called on connection
  open) public API.
- Fix format_integer() to use val.unsigned_abs() instead of val.abs()
  to avoid panic on i64::MIN.

datetime.rs:
- Fix weekday modifier: when already on the target weekday, treat as
  no-op (SQLite semantics) instead of advancing 7 days. The condition
  changed from diff <= 0 to diff < 0.
- Fix timediff_impl() millisecond overflow carry: when rounding
  produces ms >= 1000, carry into seconds (was clamping to 999).
- Fix timediff_impl() subtraction: use plain subtraction instead of
  saturating_sub, since the function already ensures start <= end and
  handles borrow cascading correctly.

lib.rs:
- Re-export get_total_changes and reset_total_changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
